title: Cache Identity, TTL, Clock Injection, Readonly Copies, and Capacity
description: The cache keys raw input, reads Date.now internally, shares mutable objects, conflates stale with absent, stores aborted promises, and has no entry or byte ceiling. Learners cumulatively produce observable bounded TypeScript resource cache through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Create a canonical typed key from endpoint, resolved identity, page parameters, and representation rather than user spelling.
Inject a clock and define fresh, stale, expired, and boundary instants without timer sleeps in unit tests.
Use immutable domain values, controlled cloning, or copy-on-read so consumers cannot rewrite cached or historical data.
